sketches.mechanism: An object's __del__ finalizer can resurrect the object by storing a new reference during destruction.
sketches.resonance: The goodbye becomes the very act that keeps the speaker here.

sketches.mechanism: A let declaration creates a temporal dead zone for its whole block, so the inner name shadows the outer one even before it is initialized.
sketches.resonance: The future name erases the present one before it can exist.

sketches.mechanism: Receiving from a closed channel returns immediately with the zero value and ok as false.
sketches.resonance: After the channel is closed, what arrives is emptiness carrying proof that no voice remains.
sketches.draft:
package main
func main() {
    silence := make(chan struct{})
    close(silence)
    _, heard := <-silence
    _ = heard
}
